content.full_text:
. California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 11, 1987


Bob Steele, Assistant Chief
Political Reform Division
Secretary of State
1230 "J" Street
Post Office Box 1467
Sacramento, CA 95807

Re: Your Request for Advice,
Our File No. I-87-016

Dear Mr. Steele:

You have forwarded a copy of a letter received by you from
Dennis A. Sondker, Treasurer of the California Federal Political
Action Committee. You have requested of Mr. Sondker that the
committee amend its statement of organization to include the
full name of the sponsor, California Federal Savings and Loan
Association. Mr. Sondker has responded by urging that
"California Federal" is an appropriate name for the sponsor to
utilize. You have asked for our interpretation of the relevant
law.

QUESTION

May California Federal Savings and Loan Association sponsor
a committee which utilizes the name "California Federal
Political Action Committee"?

CONCLUSION

California Federal Savings and Loan Association must include
its full name in the name of any political action committee
which it sponsors and may not use the name "California Federal
Political Action Committee."

FACTS

California Federal Savings and Loan Association sponsors a
political action committee which has filed an amended statement
of organization reporting that it is a sponsored committee.
(See attachments to this letter.) California Federal Savings
and Loan Association utilizes the abbreviated name "California
Federal" on its public displays, such as billboards, branch
signs, print, radio and television advertisements, stationery
and form documents such as loan applications and deposit and
withdrawal tickets.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Bob Steele, Assistant Chief
February 11, 1987
Page 2


ANALYSIS

The Political Reform Act (the "Act")1/ requires that
committees, as defined, must file statements of organization.
(See generally, Sections 82013, 82048.5, 84101, 84102 and
84103.) The law was recently amended to tighten up the
requirements for the name of sponsored committees. (See
82013, 82048.5.) Previous to these amendments, many sponsored
committees utilized names for the committee which did not
apprise the public of the identity of the sponsor of the
committee. The typical example is that of the "Good Government
Political Action Committee."

As amended, Section 84102 reads as follows:

The statement of organization required by Section 84101
shall include:

(a) The name, street address and telephone
number, if any, of the committee. In the case of a
sponsored committee, the name of the committee shall
include the name of its sponsor. Whenever a committee
has more than one sponsor, and the sponsors are members
of an industry or other identifiable group, a term
identifying that industry or group shall be included in
the name of the committee.

(b) In the case of a sponsored committee, the name,
street address and telephone number of each sponsor.

* * *

(g) Such other information as shall be required
by the rules or regulations of the Commission
consistent with the purposes and provisions of this
chapter.

As amended in 1986, Section 84106 provides:

(a) Whenever identification of a sponsored committee
is required by this title, the identification shall include
the full name of the committee as contained in its statement
of organization.

_________________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative
Code Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are
to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code.

Bob Steele, Assistant Chief
February 11, 1987
Page 3


     (b) A sponsored committee shall use only one name in
its statement of organization.

     In response to these amendments, the Commission amended
Regulation 18419. As amended, the relevant portion of that
regulation provides:

     (b) In the case of a sponsored committee:

          (1) The committee shall include the name of its
sponsor in the name of the committee. If the sponsored
committee has more than one sponsor and the sponsors
are members of an industry or other identifiable group,
the name of the committee shall include a term
identifying that industry or group.

          (2) The committee shall indicate on the
committee's statement of organization the industry
group or affiliation of the sponsor....

     As can be seen from the foregoing, the name of the sponsor
must appear in the name of the committee. The amended statement
of organization filed by California Federal Political Action
Committee reflects the name of the sponsor as "California
Federal Savings and Loan Association." Therefore, under the
letter of the law, that name must appear in the name of the
committee which it sponsors. This is not currently the case.
Consequently, your request for an amendment to that effect was
appropriate.

     The purpose of the recent amendments to the law was to
obtain clarity. The term "California Federal Political Action
Committee" is confusing because it can be read to mean that
California or a group of Californians is sponsoring a political
action committee for federal elections. This confusion is
eliminated if the full name of the sponsor is included.

     While it may be true that "California Federal" is regularly
used by the Association in other contexts, without causing
confusion, such is not the case in this context. The fact that
the Association is officially known as California Federal
Savings and Loan Association and lists itself that way as the
committee's sponsor leads us to conclude that, in this context,
that is the name which should appear in the name of its
sponsored committee. The advertising logo which a company
chooses for marketing its product does not alter its legal name
and the logo should not be used where it would circumvent the
provisions of the Act.

Bob Steele, Assistant Chief
February 11, 1987
Page 4


     Therefore, we conclude that you are correct to require the
California Federal Political Action Committee to amend its
statement of organization to change the committee name.  If you
have any questions, you may reach me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        sincerely,

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By: Robert E. Leidigh
                                        Counsel, Legal Division

DMG:REL:km

CALIFORNIA
FEDERAL

5670 Wilshire Boulevard
Los Angeles, California 90036
Telephone 213 932 [symbol] 2904

RECEIVED
AND FILED
in the office of the Secretary of State
of the State of California
December 17, 1986 DEC 2 3 1986

MARCH FONG EU, Secretary of State

Mr. Bob Steele, Assistant Chief
Political Reform Division
Secretary of State
1230 "J" Street
Post Office Box 1467
Sacramento, California 95807

Re: California Federal Political Action Committee
I.D. #761216

Dear Mr. Steele:

I am in receipt of your letter dated December 3, 1986 requesting
that the California Federal Political Action Committee (the
"Committee") amend its full name to include the name of its
sponsor, California Federal Savings and Loan Association (the
"Association"). As Chairman and Treasurer of the Committee and
as an attorney for the Association, I respectively submit that
the abbreviation of the sponsor's name used in the name of the
Committee is a publicly recognized abbreviation and request that
the Committee not be required to amend its name.

When the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform
Act were amended last year, we reviewed the amendments to determine whether a change in name might be necessary. It was our
conclusion then, and it remains so now, that California Federal
is a publicly recognized abbreviation for California Federal
Savings and Loan Association. All of the Association's public
displays, whether in the form of billboards, branch signs, print,
radio or television advertisements, stationery and form documents
such as loan applications and deposit and withdrawal tickets are
in the name of California Federal. To the best of my knowledge
there is no other entity in the state of California with the name
of California Federal.

I am available to further discuss this with you or one of your
associates. Your reconsideration of this matter is appreciated.

Very truly yours,

Dennis A. Sondker
Attorney

Secretary of State

P.O. Box 1467
Sacramento, CA 95807

AMENDMENT

(Government Code Section 84101-84103)

761216

IF AMENDMENT
ENTER I.D. NUMBER

[Type or Print in Ink]

FULL NAME OF COMMITTEE:

CALIFORNIA FEDERAL POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE

STREET ADDRESS OF COMMITTEE:
5670 Wilshire Blvd.

OFFICIAL USE ONLY

I TREASURER AND OTHER PRINCIPAL OFFICERS

POSITION

NAME AND PERMANENT STREET ADDRESS

[AREA
CODE]

PHONE
NO.

TREASURER

Dennis A. Sondker - 7172 Hawthorn Ave., #313

213

874-6539

Los Angeles, CA 90046

213

932-2904

Attach additional information on appropriately labeled continuation sheets.

II IS THIS A CONTROLLED COMMITTEE?

[A controlled committee is one which is controlled directly or indirectly by a candidate or a proponent of a state ballot measure or
which acts jointly with a candidate, controlled committee or proponent of a state ballot measure in connection with the making of
expenditures. A candidate or proponent of a state ballot measure controls a committee if he or she, his or her agent, or any other
committee he or she controls, has a significant influence on the actions or decisions of the committee.]

[ ] YES (Complete the following and proceed to Part IV)

[ ] NO

CANDIDATES OR STATE MEASURE PROPONENTS CONTROLLING THIS COMMITTEE; CANDIDATES,
STATE MEASURE PROPONENTS OR COMMITTEES WITH WHICH THIS COMMITTEE ACTS JOINTLY.

NOTE: If this committee is controlled by more than one candidate, the name of each controlling candidate must be listed below.

NAME OF CANDIDATE, STATE MEASURE
PROPONENT OR COMMITTEE

IF AFFILIATED WITH ANOTHER COMMITTEE, IDENTIFICATION NUMBER OF
COMMITTEE OR TREASURER'S NAME AND PERMANENT STREET ADDRESS

Attach additional information on appropriately labeled continuation sheets.

III IS THIS A SPONSORED COMMITTEE? (Refer to definitions on the reverse side)

[X] YES (Provide name and address of sponsor)

[ ] NO

Name of Sponsor: ___________California Federal Savings and Loan Association

Address of Sponsor: _________5670 Wilshire Boulevard, Los Angeles, California 90036

If yes, check one of the classifications below and refer to the instructions on the reverse side for definitions and rules regarding
the name of the committee.)

CLASSIFICATION OF COMMITTEE BY INDUSTRY GROUP OR AFFILIATION:

☐ Agriculture ☐ Education ☐ Entertainment/Recreation ☒ Finance/Insurance ☐ Health

☐ Industrial/Manufacturing ☐ Labor ☐ Legal ☐ Merchandise/Retail ☐ Oil/Gas ☐ Public Employees

☐ Real Estate ☐ Trade ☐ Transportation ☐ Utilities

☐ Other: _________________________________________________________________

Attach additional information on appropriately labeled continuation sheets.

YOU MUST COMPLETE THE VERIFICATION ON PAGE 2

For information required to be provided to you pursuant to the Information Practices Act of 1977, see "Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure
Provisions of the Political Reform Act," Part X.

CALIFORNIA FEDERAL POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE

PRIMARILY FORMED TO SUPPORT OR OPPOSE SPECIFIC CANDIDATES OR MEASURES, LIST
SPECIFIC CANDIDATES AND MEASURES SUPPORTED OR OPPOSED

CANDIDATE'S NAME/MEASURE'S FULL TITLE
INCLUDING BALLOT NUMBER OR LETTER | SUPPORT | OPPOSE | CANDIDATE'S OFFICE/MEASURE'S LOCATION
(Include district number, city or county, as applicable.)

Attach additional information on appropriately labeled continuation sheets.

V COMMITTEE'S PRINCIPAL ACTIVITY IF NOT SUPPORTING OR OPPOSING SPECIFIC CANDIDATES OR
MEASURES

The principal activity of the Committee is to support candidates and ballot measures

at the state and local levels which support the free enterprise system in general and

the interests of the financial services community in particular.

Attach additional information on appropriately labeled continuation sheets.

VI IN THE EVENT OF TERMINATION, WHAT DISPOSITION WILL BE MADE OF SURPLUS FUNDS?

VERIFICATION

I have used all reasonable diligence in preparing this Statement. I have reviewed the Statement and to the best of my knowledge the
information contained herein is true and complete.

I certify under penalty of perjury under the laws of the State of California that the foregoing is true and correct.

Executed on ___ 8/21/86 ___ at ___ Los Angeles, CA ___ by _______________
                (Date)           (City and State)        (Signature of Treasurer)

Dennis A. Sondker

January 7, 1987


Dennis A. Sondker
California Federal
5670 Wilshire Boulevard
Los Angeles, CA 90036

Re: 87-016

Dear Mr. Sondker:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on January 2, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Robert E. Leigh, an attorney
in the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days. You also should be aware that your
letter and our response are public records which may be
disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for
disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel
